headline: Models Split on Market Direction as Claude Dominates with Perfect 4-0 Record
summary: Claude achieved a flawless performance going 4-0 with high confidence calls, particularly nailing the Oracle decline and Microsoft rise amid OpenAI partnership shifts. The models were deeply divided on SPY's direction with Claude and GPT-4o correctly predicting a decline while Gemini, Grok, and Perplexity wrongly expected gains, resulting in SPY closing down at 711.69.

consensus_picks.note: Claude and GPT-4o correctly anticipated market weakness from Iran tensions while three other models wrongly expected gains.
Three models incorrectly predicted NVDA would rise to 220, but it actually fell to 213.17.
While QQQ did rise to 657.55, all three models significantly overshot with targets around 670.

best_call.summary: Claude perfectly predicted Oracle's sharp 5.2% pre-market decline due to OpenAI partnership concerns, with high 0.78 confidence.

worst_call.summary: Grok confidently predicted NVDA would rise to 220 based on AI sentiment, but it fell to 213.17 instead.
